content: A curiosity-driven autonomous agent can dynamically explore and revise its own numerical task and motion planning (TAMP) operators during long-horizon robot manipulation tasks, using an intrinsic curiosity module (ICM) to prioritize exploring uncertain planning constraints that conventional hand-coded TAMP frameworks never revise. This creates a self-improving TAMP system that learns to avoid unmodeled obstacles, kinematic singularities, and task execution failures without requiring labeled human demonstration data.
